title: cinchona mutisii
intro: Cinchona mutisii, also known as the yellow-berried cinchona or yellow bark, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, specifically for its bark, which contains quinine, a natural compound used to treat malaria. The health benefits of Cinchona mutisii include reducing fever, relieving muscle and joint pain, and acting as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Cinchona mutisii is often prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the dried bark in hot water, which can also be used to make a tincture or a powder for medicinal purposes.
